all.en: Min-hsuan had been the quietest student in class for almost three full years.
all.zh: 敏萱已經當了將近三年班上最安靜的學生。

all.en: She ate lunch alone, walked home alone, and rarely spoke unless a teacher asked first.
all.zh: 她獨自吃午餐、獨自走路回家，除非老師先問，否則很少開口說話。

all.en: Her favorite place in the entire school was the library's quiet third-floor corner.
all.zh: 整個學校她最喜歡的地方是圖書館三樓那個安靜的角落。

all.en: There she could read for hours without anyone noticing or asking awkward questions.
all.zh: 在那裡她可以讀好幾個小時的書，沒有人會注意她或問尷尬的問題。

all.en: One Wednesday afternoon, a new librarian named Ms. Kao quietly took over the front desk.
all.zh: 一個星期三下午，一位名叫高老師的新圖書館員悄悄接手了前台。

all.en: Ms. Kao had kind eyes, gray streaks in her hair, and a soft, patient voice.
all.zh: 高老師有著親切的眼神、髮間夾雜的灰白髮絲，以及柔和而有耐心的嗓音。

all.en: She noticed Min-hsuan come in every single afternoon and head straight to the same chair.
all.zh: 她注意到敏萱每天下午都會進來，直接走向同一張椅子。

all.en: After two weeks, Ms. Kao left a small bookmark on Min-hsuan's usual table by surprise.
all.zh: 兩週後，高老師悄悄在敏萱常坐的桌上留了一張小書籤。

all.en: It said simply, I saved a new novel for you behind the desk, in neat handwriting.
all.zh: 上面工整地寫著，我在櫃台後幫你留了一本新小說。

all.en: Min-hsuan blushed deeply but walked over and quietly accepted the gift without speaking.
all.zh: 敏萱深深地臉紅，但走過去默默地接下了禮物，一句話也沒說。

all.en: The book was about a shy girl who learned to find her voice through writing poetry.
all.zh: 那本書講述一個害羞的女孩透過寫詩找到自己聲音的故事。

all.en: Min-hsuan finished the entire novel in three nights, hidden under her blanket with a flashlight.
all.zh: 敏萱在三個晚上裡讀完整本小說，躲在棉被下用手電筒照著看。

all.en: When she returned it, Ms. Kao asked her one simple question about the main character.
all.zh: 當她還書時，高老師問了她一個關於主角的簡單問題。

all.en: Surprisingly, Min-hsuan whispered an answer that was thoughtful, detailed, and almost poetic itself.
all.zh: 出乎意料地，敏萱輕聲回答，她的答案深思熟慮、詳細，本身幾乎就是一首詩。

all.en: Their short conversations slowly became weekly, then daily, then long and full of laughter.
all.zh: 她們的短暫對話慢慢變成每週一次，再變成每天，最後變得很長且充滿笑聲。

all.en: Ms. Kao recommended one careful book at a time, choosing each one with great attention.
all.zh: 高老師一次只推薦一本書，每一本都精心挑選。

all.en: She introduced Min-hsuan to poetry, mystery novels, science writing, and travel memoirs.
all.zh: 她讓敏萱認識了詩、推理小說、科學寫作和旅行回憶錄。

all.en: After one month, she invited Min-hsuan to help organize the library's small poetry corner.
all.zh: 一個月後，她邀請敏萱幫忙整理圖書館的小型詩歌角落。

all.en: Min-hsuan happily arrived early every day, dusting shelves and arranging books by mood.
all.zh: 敏萱每天都開心地早早到，撣去書架灰塵並依心情分類擺書。

all.en: Other students began noticing the new colorful display and stopping to browse curiously.
all.zh: 其他學生開始注意到新的繽紛展示，停下來好奇地瀏覽。

all.en: Ms. Kao gently suggested that Min-hsuan write her own short poem for the bulletin board.
all.zh: 高老師溫和地建議敏萱為公布欄寫一首自己的短詩。

all.en: The idea terrified her, but she stayed up all night writing eleven different versions.
all.zh: 這個想法讓她害怕，但她熬了一整夜寫了十一個不同版本。

all.en: Her final poem was about an empty chair that learned to feel sunlight again.
all.zh: 她最終的那首詩寫的是一張空椅子重新學會感受陽光。

all.en: Ms. Kao read it slowly, looked up, and told her it deserved a much bigger audience.
all.zh: 高老師慢慢讀完，抬起頭告訴她，這首詩值得讓更多人讀到。

all.en: She secretly entered the poem in the city's annual high school writing competition.
all.zh: 她偷偷把這首詩投稿到市政府年度高中寫作比賽。

all.en: Three weeks later, an official envelope arrived addressed personally to Min-hsuan at school.
all.zh: 三週後，一個官方信封寄到學校，收件人正是敏萱。

all.en: She had won third place, and her name would appear in the city's annual writing book.
all.zh: 她贏得了第三名，她的名字將出現在市政府年度寫作集裡。

all.en: The principal announced her name proudly in front of the entire morning assembly.
all.zh: 校長在全校晨會前驕傲地宣布了她的名字。

all.en: Min-hsuan walked to the stage with shaking knees but a small, real smile on her face.
all.zh: 敏萱雙膝顫抖地走上台，臉上掛著小小但真誠的微笑。

all.en: Classmates who had never spoken to her now wanted to read her award-winning poem.
all.zh: 從未跟她說過話的同學現在都想讀她得獎的詩。

all.en: Two girls from her literature class invited her to start a quiet writing group together.
all.zh: 她文學課的兩位女同學邀請她一起組一個安靜的寫作小組。

all.en: Min-hsuan agreed, surprising herself, and they met every Friday in that same library corner.
all.zh: 敏萱答應了，連她自己都很意外，她們每週五都在圖書館同一個角落聚會。

all.en: On graduation day, she gave Ms. Kao a small handwritten book of her thirty best poems.
all.zh: 畢業那天，她送給高老師一本手寫的小書，收錄了她最好的三十首詩。

all.en: Ms. Kao hugged her gently and told her that quiet voices often carry the loudest truths.
all.zh: 高老師輕輕擁抱她，告訴她安靜的聲音常常承載著最響亮的真理。

all.en: Years later, Min-hsuan returned as a published author and signed books in that same corner.
all.zh: 多年後，敏萱以出版作家的身分回到那個相同的角落為書迷簽書。
